raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/year>, I paid {$2500.00} to a dating service called XXXX XXXX XXXX using my American Express card. I disputed this charge because the service was materially misrepresented and failed to deliver services as agreed. 

At enrollment, I was told matchmaking would be tailored to my stated preferences and that I would have final discretion over matches. Once enrolled, the company disregarded these terms, pressured me to accept matches based on their own criteria, and ignored my feedback. This was not the service I was promised. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX I submitted a written cancellation and requested a refund due to non-performance and misrepresentation. The merchant refused, citing a no refund clause in their contract. American Express denied my initial dispute for the same reason. 

I then submitted a detailed resubmission with supporting evidence showing : Clear documentation of my cancellation Communications proving ongoing attempts to provide services after cancellation Sections XXXX and XXXX of the contract allowing pro-rated refunds in cases of non-performance American Express denied the dispute again, focusing only on the no refund clause and failing to address the new evidence or my rights under Regulation Z of the Truth in Lending Act, which protects consumers from charges for services that are misrepresented or not delivered as agreed. 

I am requesting that the CFPB require American Express to : Reopen and reinvestigate this dispute Refund the {$2500.00} charge based on misrepresentation and failure to perform Ensure proper handling of disputes involving Regulation Z protections Supporting documents, including the denial letter, contract excerpt, and cancellation evidence, are attached.
